framework_selection.config_management: YAML + dataclasses (no Hydra overhead needed for this scope)
framework_selection.reasoning: Paper used CUDA-C (cudamat/cuda-convnet); PyTorch is the contemporary standard equivalent. Inverted dropout convention (scale by 1/p at train time) in torch.nn.Dropout is mathematically equivalent to paper's test-time weight scaling (scale by p) — this will be documented explicitly. No transformers or exotic ops needed.

models/.dropout_rbm.py: DropoutRBM — bonus: Restricted Boltzmann Machine variant (Section 8)

data/.dataset.py: MNISTDataModule — download, split, normalize, return DataLoaders

training/.trainer.py: Trainer — SGD+momentum loop, max-norm projection, LR decay, checkpointing

utils/.config.py: DropoutConfig dataclass — all hyperparameters with defaults matching paper
utils/.max_norm.py: apply_max_norm_constraint() — projects weight vectors onto L2 ball

configs/.mnist_3layer_1024.yaml: Primary repro target: 3-layer 1024-unit ReLU, p=0.5/0.8, c=2, momentum=0.95
configs/.mnist_ablation_dropout_rate.yaml: Fig 9 ablation: vary p from 0.1 to 1.0
configs/.mnist_ablation_dataset_size.yaml: Fig 10 ablation: vary training set size
configs/.mnist_regularizer_comparison.yaml: Table 9: L2 / L2+L1 / KL-sparsity / max-norm / dropout+L2 / dropout+max-norm

classes.name: DropoutNet
classes.description: Configurable L-layer feed-forward neural network with Bernoulli dropout, ReLU activations, and optional max-norm constraint. Supports both inverted-dropout (train-time scaling, PyTorch default) and paper-style (test-time weight scaling). Primary repro target: 3 layers, 1024 units, p_hidden=0.5, p_input=0.8.

constructor_args.input_dim: int — input feature dimension (784 for MNIST)
constructor_args.hidden_dims: List[int] — list of hidden layer widths e.g. [1024, 1024, 1024]
constructor_args.p_hidden: float — dropout retention probability for hidden layers (default 0.5)
constructor_args.p_input: float — dropout retention probability for input layer (default 0.8)
constructor_args.use_dropout: bool — toggle for ablation comparison (default True)

public_methods: get_hidden_activations(x: Tensor[B, D_in]) -> List[Tensor[B, D_l]]  # for sparsity analysis
apply_weight_scaling(p: float) -> None  # paper-style test-time scaling (optional, for verification)
internal_components: self.input_dropout: nn.Dropout(p=1-p_input)  # note: nn.Dropout takes drop prob, not retain prob
classes.notes: PyTorch nn.Dropout(p) drops with probability p and scales retained activations by 1/(1-p) during training. Paper defines p as RETENTION probability. Therefore nn.Dropout should receive (1 - p_paper) as its argument. This inversion is critical and must be documented with a comment in the code.

classes.name: DropoutRBM
classes.description: RBM with binary visible and hidden units. Dropout mask r ~ Bernoulli(p) applied per training case to hidden units. CD-1 contrastive divergence training.

constructor_args.n_hidden: int — hidden unit count (paper uses 256 for MNIST feature analysis)
constructor_args.p_hidden: float — retention probability for hidden units (default 0.5)

public_methods: sample_hidden(v: Tensor[B, D]) -> Tuple[Tensor[B, F], Tensor[B, F]]  # probs and binary samples
sample_visible(h: Tensor[B, F]) -> Tuple[Tensor[B, D], Tensor[B, D]]
contrastive_divergence(v: Tensor[B, D], k: int = 1) -> Dict[str, Tensor]  # returns weight gradients
forward(v: Tensor[B, D]) -> Tensor[B, F]  # returns hidden activation probabilities

classes.name: MNISTDataModule
classes.description: Handles MNIST download, validation split (10K from 60K train), normalization, DataLoader creation.

constructor_args.val_size: int — validation split size (10000, per Appendix B.1)

public_methods: combined_dataloader() -> DataLoader  # 60K (train+val) for final training per Appendix B.1

sir_mapping: training_pipeline — SGD, momentum, lr schedule, max-norm, checkpointing

classes.name: Trainer
classes.description: Full training loop. SGD with momentum, optional LR decay, max-norm projection after every gradient step. Tracks train/val error over weight-update steps (not epochs) to match paper's Figure 4 x-axis.
public_methods: train(train_loader, val_loader, n_updates: int) -> Dict[str, List[float]]  # returns loss/error histories
evaluate(loader: DataLoader) -> Dict[str, float]  # returns {'error_rate': float, 'loss': float}
internal_logic: After each optimizer.step(), call apply_max_norm_constraint() on all hidden layer weight matrices
Log metrics every 1000 weight updates (to replicate Figure 4 trajectories)
Two-phase training per Appendix B.1: (1) tune on 50K+val split → pick hyperparams, (2) retrain on full 60K for 1M updates

functions.name: apply_max_norm_constraint
functions.description: Iterates over all Linear layers in model. For each weight row w_i (incoming weights to unit i), if ||w_i||_2 > c, rescales: w_i = w_i * c / ||w_i||_2. Applied in-place after every gradient update. Does NOT apply to output layer bias or to the output layer itself.
pseudocode: for each nn.Linear layer (excluding output layer):
scale = clamp(c / norms, max=1.0)  # don't scale up, only down
W = W * scale.unsqueeze(1)  # broadcast and apply

classes.name: DropoutConfig
classes.description: Frozen dataclass holding all hyperparameters. Loadable from YAML. All values directly from SIR with confidence annotations as inline comments.

classes.name: ModelConfig
fields: p_hidden: float = 0.5  # SIR confidence 0.95 — paper states p=0.5 for hidden
p_input: float = 0.8   # SIR confidence 0.95 — paper states p=0.8 for input
max_norm_c: float = 2.0  # SIR confidence 0.95 — Appendix B.1 states c=2

classes.name: TrainingConfig
fields: learning_rate: float = 0.01  # SIR confidence 0.65 — ASSUMED, not stated for MNIST
momentum: float = 0.95  # SIR confidence 0.95 — Appendix B.1
weight_decay: float = 0.0  # dropout+max-norm replaces L2 in primary config
n_weight_updates: int = 1000000  # SIR confidence 0.95 — Appendix B.1
val_check_interval: int = 1000  # log every N weight updates

classes.name: DataConfig
fields: val_size: int = 10000  # SIR confidence 0.97 — Appendix B.1 explicit

functions.name: compute_error_rate
functions.description: Classification error rate as percentage: (1 - accuracy) * 100

functions.name: compute_sparsity_stats
functions.description: Compute mean activation and fraction of activations near zero — for Figure 8 / Section 7.2 analysis

functions.name: compute_regularizer_comparison
functions.description: Format Table 9 style comparison across regularization methods

dropout_net_training_forward_pass.description: Forward pass of DropoutNet during training (masks active)
steps: STEP 1: x = input_dropout(x)         → [B, 784]  (Bernoulli mask p_input=0.8, drops 20%)
STEP 4: x = hidden_dropout_0(x)     → [B, 1024] (Bernoulli mask p_hidden=0.5, drops 50%)
dropout_net_training_forward_pass.notes: nn.Dropout is only active during model.train(). Calling model.eval() disables all masks — no manual weight scaling needed with inverted dropout convention.

dropout_net_inference_forward_pass.description: Forward pass at test time (all dropout disabled, weights unmodified)
steps: STEP 2-10: same linear+relu sequence with NO masking
dropout_net_inference_forward_pass.notes: PyTorch inverted dropout scales at train time, so test-time weights are already at the correct scale. Equivalent to paper's W_test = p * W.

steps: For layer l in {linear_0, linear_1, linear_2}  (NOT output_linear):
norms = ||W||_2 per row            → [D_out]    (norm of incoming weights per unit)
STEP 1: r ~ Bernoulli(p)            → [B, F_hidden]   (dropout mask, re-sampled per example)
STEP 2: h_probs = sigmoid(v_0 @ W + b) * r  → [B, F]
STEP 3: h_0 = Bernoulli(h_probs)    → [B, F]  (positive phase sample)
STEP 4: v_1_probs = sigmoid(h_0 @ W.T + a)  → [B, D]
STEP 5: v_1 = Bernoulli(v_1_probs)  → [B, D]  (negative phase reconstruction)
STEP 6: h_1_probs = sigmoid(v_1 @ W + b) * r  → [B, F]  (same mask reused)
DELTA W = (v_0.T @ h_probs - v_1.T @ h_1_probs) / B

training.learning_rate: 0.01  # ASSUMED: not stated for MNIST; typical range for momentum SGD

target_result.description: Table 2, Row: Dropout NN + max-norm, ReLU, 3 layers 1024 units

cli_args: --config   str   path to YAML config file (default: configs/mnist_3layer_1024.yaml)
--run-name str   experiment name for checkpoint dir
--no-dropout     flag: disable dropout for baseline comparison
--resume   str   path to checkpoint to resume from

entrypoints.description: Evaluate a trained checkpoint on test set
cli_args: --checkpoint str  path to saved model checkpoint
--report-sparsity  flag: also compute hidden activation sparsity (Section 7.2)
entrypoints.outputs: prints error rate to stdout; optionally saves sparsity histograms

entrypoints.description: Run inference on a single image
cli_args: --checkpoint str  path to saved model checkpoint
--image      str  path to input image (28x28 PNG or JPG)
entrypoints.outputs: predicted digit class and softmax probabilities

cli_args: --ablation dropout-rate  runs sweep over p in {0.1,0.2,...,1.0}
--ablation dataset-size  runs sweep over dataset sizes {100,500,1K,5K,10K,50K}
--ablation regularizers  runs all Table 9 regularization methods

dependency_rationale.torch + torchvision: Core DL framework; torchvision for MNIST dataset and transforms
dependency_rationale.numpy: Array ops, histogram computation for sparsity analysis
dependency_rationale.pytest: Unit tests for max-norm, dropout mask, tensor shapes

risk_assessment.title: Learning Rate Not Specified for MNIST
risk_assessment.description: Paper states dropout nets need '10-100x the learning rate of a standard net' but gives no absolute value for MNIST. SIR confidence 0.65. Assumed lr=0.01.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Config is YAML-parametric; include lr sweep in ablation script. Document assumption prominently in README. Expected error range 0.9-1.2% is achievable with lr in {0.001, 0.01, 0.1} with proper momentum.

risk_assessment.title: Batch Size Not Specified
risk_assessment.description: No batch size stated for MNIST experiments. Assumed 128. SIR confidence 0.65.
risk_assessment.mitigation: 128 is a very common default; config allows override. 1M weight updates with batch 128 = ~2133 epochs over 50K samples — training time ~30min on RTX 3050.

risk_assessment.title: PyTorch Inverted Dropout Convention
risk_assessment.description: Paper defines p as retention probability and scales weights DOWN at test time. PyTorch nn.Dropout takes DROP probability and scales activations UP at train time. These are mathematically equivalent but the sign of the argument is inverted.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add assert and docstring in DropoutNet constructor: 'p_hidden=0.5 means 50% retained. Passed to nn.Dropout as (1 - p_hidden) = 0.5 drop probability.' Add a unit test verifying expected activation scale.

risk_assessment.title: 1M Weight Updates Is Very Long
risk_assessment.description: Paper trains for 1M weight updates (Appendix B.1). With batch=128 on MNIST, this is ~2133 epochs. On CPU this would take hours. On RTX 3050 ~25-40 minutes.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add --quick-run flag for 100K updates (sufficient to observe convergence trend). Default config targets full 1M for exact result reproduction.

risk_assessment.title: DBM Pretraining Not Reproduced
risk_assessment.description: Best MNIST result (0.79%) requires Deep Boltzmann Machine pretraining — a separate multi-stage training pipeline not in scope.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Primary target is 1.06% (Table 2, no pretraining). DropoutRBM module provides building block. README documents this scope decision explicitly.

risk_assessment.title: Max-Norm Applied to All Hidden Layers Including Convolutional
risk_assessment.description: For conv net variants, max-norm applies to conv filter weights. Our primary target is FC-only, so this is not a current blocker.
risk_assessment.mitigation: apply_max_norm_constraint() checks layer type; only applies to nn.Linear in primary target. A separate apply_conv_max_norm() can be added for future SVHN/CIFAR reproduction.

test_plan.description: Verify that with model.train(), output mean is scaled correctly; with model.eval(), no scaling occurs
After apply_max_norm_constraint, all hidden weight row norms must be <= c
use_dropout=False produces deterministic, identical outputs on two forward passes
Train for 5K updates on MNIST; verify error < 5% (sanity, not paper-level)
